title: Not Possible to Use Multiple slirp4netns Networks
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind feature

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

I would like to create a rootless container with multiple slirp tun devices, so that I can use the `outbound_addr=INTERFACE` option to route outbound traffic to specific interfaces within the container.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `podman run --network=slirp4netns:outbound_addr=en1 --network=slirp4netns:outbound_addr=en2 -- debian ls /sys/class/net`

**Describe the results you received:**

1. Notice that there is only `lo` and `tun0`
2. Check `podman inspect` to see that only one network is configured (as far as I can tell, the last `--network` option passed takes precedence

**Describe the results you expected:**
There would be two `tun?` devices configured, two instances of `slirp4netns` running with the appropriate `--outbound-addr` option, two networks would be visible in `podman inspect`

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
An alternative solution using networks explicitly created with `podman network create` and/or manually configuring the CNI network definition would work for me as well. (But I looked at the CNI docs and couldn't see a way to specify the outbound interface for a bridge network.)


**Output of `podman version`:**

```
$ podman version
Version:      3.0.1
API Version:  3.0.0
Go Version:   go1.15.9
Built:        Thu Jan  1 00:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

(I know it's old, but that's what I've got on Debian 11)

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
$ podman info --debug

    rootless: true
    seccompEnabled: true
    selinuxEnabled: false
  slirp4netns:
    executable: /home/scott/slirp4netns/slirp4netns

  swapFree: 17179865088

  uptime: 47h 40m 14.48s (Approximately 1.96 days)

      Version: |-
        fusermount3 version: 3.10.3
        fuse-overlayfs: version 1.4
        FUSE library version 3.10.3
        using FUSE kernel interface version 7.31
```

(Notice that I'm using a newer version of slirp4netns than is available in the Debian repos to get the `outbound_addr` feature.)

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman/stable,now 3.0.1+dfsg1-3+b2 amd64 [installed]
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**

No--I have not tried an updated Podman, but I don't believe this feature has been implemented from a reading of the release announcements

Yes--I have read the troubleshooting guide

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Physical machine

comments.body:
I do not think this is possible. What is your goal here?

Even if there would be two slirp4netns interfaces in the container with different outbound interfaces the container still has only one default route so all the traffic would only be routed through one of them.

In my particular case, I would like to configure dnsdist to connect to an upstream DNS server via a specific interface without altering the host's routing table (https://dnsdist.org/guides/downstreams.html#source-address-selection).

However, in general, it would also be possible to configure a routing table inside the container, would it not? For example, if you wanted to use a different default route in the container than on the host.

When you say this is not possible, do you mean truly impossible given the constraints on rootless networking in the current Linux Kernel or not possible without changes to Podman?

Well it is possible from the kernel point of view but I really do not see how podman could expose this. I also do not know if slirp4netns supports this but I see no reason why it would not support it.

The problem is that I see no good way to integrate this into podman, there can only be one default gateway. Altering the routing table in the container netns to support some special rules is out of scope IMO.
You can always use your own custom network namespace via --network ns:/path/to/netns
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Closing since I consider this out of scope for podman. You can always setup the network namespace yourself and use it via `--network ns:/path/to/netns`
